mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 1.0 percent signals extreme monetary easing that compresses equity discount rates and reduces borrowing costs, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment crossing above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that reduces household consumption and corporate earnings, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of cases when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, triggering broad risk-off selling and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not predict negative 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or positive returns occur in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month drops in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signal immediate manufacturing stress and supply chain disruption, causing short-term risk-off equity selling.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following an INDPRO drop greater than 1.0 percent, or signal loses predictive power in stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated while policy rates approach zero, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity rotation into growth assets and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative returns dominate when FEDFUNDS is below 1.5 and CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent payroll declines reflect weakening economic activity that reduces future corporate revenue expectations, driving negative medium-term equity returns across market cycles.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contraction confirms recessionary conditions, leading investors to price in lower earnings growth and sell equities over the next month as macro uncertainty rises.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of quarters with negative GDP growth, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production falls while real sales remain stable signals inventory drawdown rather than demand collapse, supporting short-term equity resilience and positive returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal of falling INDPRO and stable RSAFS yields negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
